title: Ensure That Separation of Duties Is Enforced While Assigning  Service Account Related Roles to Users

input_row.title: Ensure That Separation of Duties Is Enforced While Assigning  Service Account Related Roles to Users
input_row.description: It is recommended that the principle of 'Separation of Duties' is enforced while assigning service-account related roles to users.
input_row.rationale: The built-in/predefined IAM role Service Account admin allows the user/identity to create, delete, and manage service account(s). The built-in/predefined IAM role Service Account User allows the user/identity (with adequate privileges on Compute and App Engine) to assign service account(s) to Apps/Compute Instances. Separation of duties is the concept of ensuring that one individual does not have all necessary permissions to be able to complete a malicious action. In Cloud IAM - service accounts, this could be an action such as using a service account to access resources that user should not normally have access to. Separation of duties is a business control typically used in larger organizations, meant to help avoid security or privacy incidents and errors. It is considered best practice. No user should have Service Account Admin and Service Account User roles assigned at the same time.
input_row.audit: From Google Cloud Console 1. Go to IAM & Admin/IAM using https://console.cloud.google.com/iam- admin/iam. 2. Ensure no member has the roles Service Account Admin and Service account User assigned together. From Google Cloud CLI 1. List all users and role assignments: gcloud projects get-iam-policy [Project_ID] --format json | \ jq -r '[ (["Service_Account_Admin_and_User"] | (., map(length*"-"))), ( [ .bindings[] | select(.role == "roles/iam.serviceAccountAdmin" or .role == "roles/iam.serviceAccountUser").members[] ] | group_by(.) | map({User: ., Count: length}) | .[] | select(.Count == 2).User | unique ) ] | .[] | @tsv' 2. All common users listed under Service_Account_Admin_and_User are assigned both the roles/iam.serviceAccountAdmin and roles/iam.serviceAccountUser roles.
input_row.remediation: From Google Cloud Console 1. Go to IAM & Admin/IAM using https://console.cloud.google.com/iam- admin/iam. 2. For any member having both Service Account Admin and Service account User roles granted/assigned, click the Delete Bin icon to remove either role from the member. Removal of a role should be done based on the business requirements.
input_row.impact: The removed role should be assigned to a different user based on business needs.
input_row.additional_information: Users granted with Owner (roles/owner) and Editor (roles/editor) have privileges equivalent to Service Account Admin and Service Account User. To avoid the misuse, Owner and Editor roles should be granted to very limited users and Use of these primitive privileges should be minimal. These requirements are addressed in separate recommendations.

step1_initial_assessment:
**AUDIT APPROACH:** Automated

**APPROACH DECISION:**
The control pertains to IAM role assignments concerning service accounts, which can indeed be queried using GCP APIs and the Google Cloud SDK. The audit field includes clear instructions on how to check the configuration using `gcloud` commands, thus confirming that this control can be automated. 

**PROGRAM NAME:** gcp_iam_service_account_separation_of_duties_enforced

**AUTOMATION DETAILS:**
The automated program would check the following:

1. **GCP API Calls and Google Cloud SDK Methods:**
   - The program will utilize the `gcloud` command with the `.get-iam-policy` method to retrieve IAM policies for a specific project.

2. **GCP CLI Commands:**
   - Command: 
     ```
     gcloud projects get-iam-policy [Project_ID] --format json
     ```
   - This command retrieves all IAM roles and their associated members for the specified project.

3. **Specific Resources to Enumerate:**
   - The resource to enumerate is the list of members who have roles assigned at the project level that include `roles/iam.serviceAccountAdmin` and `roles/iam.serviceAccountUser`.

4. **Exact Configuration Properties/Settings to Validate:**
   - The program will look for members assigned both `roles/iam.serviceAccountAdmin` and `roles/iam.serviceAccountUser`, identifying any violations of the separation of duties principle.

5. **Clear Pass/Fail Criteria with Expected Values:**
   - **Pass:** No member is found with both roles assigned simultaneously.
   - **Fail:** At least one member is found with both `roles/iam.serviceAccountAdmin` and `roles/iam.serviceAccountUser` roles.

6. **Example Check:**
   - The program will check the output from the `gcloud projects get-iam-policy` command through a script to see if the same user exists under both roles:
     ```
     jq -r '[([\"Service_Account_Admin_and_User\"] | (., map(length*\"-\"))), ( [ .bindings[] | select(.role == \"roles/iam.serviceAccountAdmin\" or .role == \"roles/iam.serviceAccountUser\").members[] ] | group_by(.) | map({User: ., Count: length}) | .[] | select(.Count == 2).User | unique ) ] | .[] | @tsv'
     ```
   - This will identify users assigned both roles, affecting compliance. If no common user is identified, the audit passes.
step2_review:
**APPROACH VALIDATION:** AGREE

- The classification of the audit approach as Automated is correct. The control can be effectively checked using GCP APIs and the Google Cloud SDK (gcloud). The use of the `gcloud projects get-iam-policy` command is appropriate for retrieving IAM policies and checking role assignments, which aligns with the capabilities of the GCP SDK and CLI.

**TECHNICAL ACCURACY:** 8

- The automation details are mostly technically correct. The `gcloud projects get-iam-policy` command is valid for retrieving IAM policies at the project level. The use of `jq` for processing JSON output is a common practice for parsing and analyzing the data.
- The program name `gcp_iam_service_account_separation_of_duties_enforced` is descriptive and clear, but could be more concise.
- The example check using `jq` is technically sound, but the explanation could be clearer in terms of how the script processes the data to identify violations.

**IMPROVEMENTS:**

- **Program Name:** Consider a more concise name such as `gcp_iam_service_account_sod_check` for clarity and brevity.
- **Explanation Clarity:** Provide a more detailed explanation of the `jq` command to ensure that users understand how it identifies users with both roles.
- **Additional Considerations:** Consider checking for other roles that might also violate the principle of separation of duties, such as `roles/iam.serviceAccountTokenCreator`, which could also be relevant depending on the organization's policy.
- **Alternative Methods:** While the current method is effective, consider using the IAM Policy Analyzer in GCP for more comprehensive analysis if available.

**CONFIDENCE:** HIGH

- The assessment is well-grounded in GCP's capabilities and best practices for IAM role management. The automated approach is appropriate and technically feasible.

**FINAL RECOMMENDATION:** Modify

- Modify the program name for conciseness.
- Enhance the explanation of the `jq` command for clarity.
- Consider additional roles that might impact separation of duties, depending on organizational context.

step3_final_decision.decision_text:
**FINAL APPROACH:** Automated

**JUSTIFICATION:** Both AI opinions agree that the control can be automated using GCP APIs and the Google Cloud SDK. The `gcloud projects get-iam-policy` command is capable of retrieving IAM policies and checking role assignments, which aligns with the requirements of the control to ensure separation of duties. The automation process can efficiently identify any violations of this principle by analyzing the roles assigned to users.

**CONFIDENCE:** HIGH

---

**PROGRAM NAME:** 
gcp_iam_service_account_sod_check

**AUTOMATION DETAILS:**
- GCP Resource/Service: IAM Roles
- API/CLI Method: Google Cloud SDK (gcloud) `projects get-iam-policy`
- Properties to Check: Members with `roles/iam.serviceAccountAdmin` and `roles/iam.serviceAccountUser`
- Pass Criteria: No member has both roles assigned simultaneously
- Fail Criteria: Any member is found with both `roles/iam.serviceAccountAdmin` and `roles/iam.serviceAccountUser` assigned

---

**DISAGREEMENTS RESOLVED:** There were no disagreements between the AI opinions; both concurred on the feasibility of automation using GCP APIs.

**SOURCE:** CIS_Google_Cloud_Platform_Foundation_Benchmark_v4_0_0
